description: Grounded and sustainable upgrade suggestions used during TRUE AI Discovery synthesis. Suggestions are designed to encourage gradual movement, reduced overload, and emotionally sustainable adjustment rather than optimization or performance escalation.

capacity_support.suggestion: Identify one recurring responsibility that may benefit from reduction, delay, or renegotiation of elements.
Create a small recurring period of uninterrupted recovery or emotional quiet each week.
Begin noticing which environments or interactions most consistently increase emotional depletion.
Experiment with a slightly slower pace in one area of life that currently feels rushed or unsustainable.
Reintroduce one activity that feels emotionally restorative rather than purely productive.

boundaries.suggestion: Practice saying 'not right now' in situations where immediate agreement is not necessary.
Choose one responsibility that may no longer need to be carried entirely alone.
Notice where guilt appears when protecting your own energy or emotional needs.
Reflect on which relationships feel energizing versus consistently depleting.
Allow one small area of life to remain unfinished, delayed, or imperfect temporarily.

alignment.suggestion: Spend time identifying which current commitments still feel emotionally meaningful and which feel primarily obligation-based.
Choose one decision this week using alignment rather than urgency as the primary filter.
Notice where your behavior feels most emotionally natural versus performative.
Pay attention to which environments support steadiness, spaciousness, or emotional honesty.
Write briefly about what a more sustainable version of your current life might realistically look like.

self_awareness.suggestion: Begin noticing recurring emotional or behavioral patterns without immediately trying to fix them.
Track how your body or emotions signal that pressure has been building too long.
Practice naming emotional experiences more specifically instead of minimizing or dismissing them.
Create a recurring space for reflection that is not focused entirely on productivity or planning.
Allow conflicting needs or emotions to coexist temporarily without forcing immediate resolution.

momentum.suggestion: Choose one small adjustment that feels realistic enough to sustain consistently.
Reduce unnecessary complexity in one area of life that currently feels emotionally heavy.
Approach change as experimentation rather than permanent reinvention.
Prioritize steadier movement over intense bursts of motivation.
Ask regularly whether your current pace still feels emotionally and practically sustainable.

support.suggestion: Identify one person, relationship, or environment where you feel more emotionally understood or supported.
Consider where support, delegation, or collaboration may reduce unnecessary strain.
Allow yourself to acknowledge difficulty without immediately turning it into self-criticism.
Focus first on creating steadiness before pursuing major transformation.
Practice responding to emotional difficulty with curiosity rather than harsh evaluation.
